title: practical english usage - Part 97
content:
and wharves are more common than the plurals in -fs.

Dwarf hoof scarf and wharf can have plurals in either -fs or -ves. Hooves, scarves loaf      loaves life      lives            wolf     wolves leaf     leaves           wife     wives knife     knives           thief     thieves half     halves           shelf     shelves elf      elves sheaf     sheaves calves           self      selves Singular       Plural                                    Singular       Plural The  following nouns ending in -f(e) have plurals in -ves.

1  irregular       plurals     in  -ves 117 irreg    u  la  r  a  nd   special     plurals mosquito   mosquito(e)s        volcano   voleano(e)s cargo     eargo(e)s         tornado   tornado(e)s buffalo    buffalo(e)s        motto     motto(e)s Singular       Plural                                    Singular       Plural The  following nouns can have plurals in -s or -es; -es is more common.

potato   potatoes         veto      vetoes hero      heroes           torpedo   torpedoes echo      echoes           tomato    tomatoes Singular       Plural                                    Singular       Plural Some nouns ending in -o have plurals in -es. The  most common: Nouns     and   Noun    Phrases;     Agreement        Section  11 grammar *117   irregular and special plurals corpus     corpora (Latin) or cactus     cacti (Latin)     cactuses (less common) basis      bases (Greek) bacterium   bacteria (Latin) appendix   appendices (Latin) analysis    analyses (Latin) Singular             Plural Some words which  come  from foreign languages have special plurals. Dice (used in board games) is originally the plural of an old noun die. In modem have plurals without -s in some kinds of expressions. For details, ►    322.14.
